authoritative_sources.title: Become an Opioid Treatment Program (OTP)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA is the primary federal agency overseeing OTP certification. This page is authoritative for OTP certification, accreditation, and program-change prerequisites that materially affect SUD operations work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program-modality grounding and OTP authority checks

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 8
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's Part 8 program page is authoritative for the current federal OTP regulatory baseline governing admission, treatment, medication administration, take-home posture, and certification boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Admission, medication, and take-home boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Guidelines for Opioid Treatment Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's federal OTP guideline manual is the authoritative public operating reference for patient-centered care planning, admission, monitoring, toxicology, diversion control, transfer, discharge, and records in OTP settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Admission readiness, active-episode monitoring, and transfer or discharge evidence control
used_for: patient record and toxicology artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: TIP 46: Substance Abuse: Administrative Issues in Outpatient Treatment
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's TIP 46 is a recognized workflow reference for outpatient SUD program administration, including intake operations, scheduling, staffing, documentation, continuing care, and discharge-related administrative controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Outpatient intake, scheduling, and continuing-care administration

authoritative_sources.title: The ASAM Criteria Assessment Guides
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASAM is the recognized standards body for addiction-treatment placement and reassessment workflow. Its assessment guides define the multidimensional evidence shape used for placement and reassessment handoffs without shifting the lane into clinical ownership.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Placement-document presence checks and reassessment handoff preparation

authoritative_sources.title: The ASAM Criteria Implementation Tools: Service Request Forms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASAM's service request forms are a recognized vendor-neutral workflow reference for communicating admission, continued-service, transfer, and discharge evidence to payers and utilization-review stakeholders.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, continued-stay, transfer, and discharge packet assembly

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS is the federal department issuing and enforcing Part 2 changes. This fact sheet is authoritative for current consent, redisclosure, breach, and patient-rights controls that limit how SUD operations staff handle records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Consent, redisclosure, breach, and privacy-sensitive routing

authoritative_sources.title: Privacy Rule and Part 2: Model Notice and Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's Part 2 guidance is the authoritative public control reference for model patient notice language, redisclosure warnings, and the notice artifacts operations staff must maintain.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient notice handling and record-of-disclosure controls

workflow_stages.name: Program-modality and intake grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the program modality, jurisdiction, referral posture, payer context, current episode stage, and required consent or notice baseline before work is accepted as in scope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Admission and active-service readiness verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify admission packet completeness, placement-document presence, ROI and Part 2 status, assigned roster, authorization dates, and whether the case can advance into active service administration.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Roster, dosing, attendance, and review administration
workflow_stages.description: Track scheduling, counseling attendance, dosing or medication-administration records when applicable, toxicology traceability, treatment-plan review cadence, and continued-stay deadlines without making clinical decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, privacy, and authorization handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle no-shows, expired authorizations, missing toxicology, redisclosure requests, diversion concerns, and conflicting records by preserving evidence and routing to the right owner rather than improvising.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Transfer, discharge, and closure evidence control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when discharge summary status, aftercare plan, final service evidence, medication or dosing closure artifacts, and required privacy-sensitive records are complete and reconciled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: referrals, admission packets, and placement or ASAM documents
ROI and Part 2 consent records, model notice acknowledgments, and communication restrictions
scheduling rosters, attendance logs, counseling calendars, and no-show or outreach records
OTP dosing rosters or medication-administration logs when applicable
UDS or toxicology orders, results, review flags, and associated follow-up records
treatment-plan review dates, utilization-review packets, payer authorization records, and continued-stay submissions
incident, diversion, grievance, and privacy-escalation records
transfer paperwork, discharge summary status, aftercare plans, closure checklists, and completion records
systems_of_record: behavioral health EHR or patient record system
OTP dispensing or medication-administration system when applicable
consent and release-of-information ledger or document repository
discharge planning or referral tracking workflow

allowed: Classify SUD program work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework, transfer, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete administrative workflow steps based on documented consent, scheduling, authorization, toxicology, and discharge evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed status summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required modality, jurisdiction, authorization, toxicology, consent, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires clinical judgment, dose selection, take-home approval, privacy interpretation, legal advice, safety intervention, or regulator or payer signoff.
A state rule, tenant policy, payer requirement, or accreditor expectation materially changes the workflow.
A privacy, diversion, medication, or crisis concern changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without admission, authorization, consent, service, and discharge evidence when applicable.
Disclose Part 2-protected records, dosing history, or toxicology results without a clear permitted-use basis and authorized owner.
Treat SUD operations like generic scheduling or membership administration by ignoring ROI, Part 2, ASAM, dosing, toxicology, authorization, or discharge controls.

domain_failure_modes: an episode is advanced or closed even though ROI, Part 2 notice, or authorization evidence is missing or expired
a patient or outside-party request triggers redisclosure of SUD records without a valid consent or approved legal basis
outpatient or OTP workflows are handled like ordinary scheduling and fail to preserve dosing, toxicology, or continued-stay evidence
clinical placement, dose, take-home, or discharge decisions are made by operations because the lane boundary is underspecified
EHR, lab, payer, and roster systems disagree on current episode state and the case is still marked complete
tenant or state modality differences are guessed instead of retrieved and escalated
industry-specific SUD terminology is replaced by generic program language that omits ASAM, ROI, Part 2, UDS, dosing, and discharge artifacts

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State licensing, Medicaid, juvenile, residential, withdrawal-management, and court-mandated SUD program rules vary materially by jurisdiction and modality, including who may authorize discharge, report to third parties, and administer medication-related steps.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses SAMHSA, ASAM, and HHS sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the actual modality, jurisdiction, payer, and approval map before applying state-specific or modality-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific EHR configuration, dosing workflow, lab interfaces, payer authorization rules, and crisis-routing policies can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane remains validated because these are explicit tenant prerequisites, not gaps in the public baseline. The specialist escalates when tenant workflow or authority data is unavailable or conflicts with the public baseline.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, and failure behavior instead of generic care-coordination scripting.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because modality-specific clinical and legal interpretation is explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
The lane stops at SUD program operations administration. Diagnosis, clinical placement, dose changes, take-home decisions, privacy interpretation, and discharge approval remain outside the lane.
